checks.a_analysis: The output document faithfully follows the text of the source PDF. No additional paragraphs, headings, or sentences have been fabricated. All text elements correspond directly to content present in the original resolution.
The document contains no contact information such as phone numbers, emails, or mailing addresses. No such information was fabricated in the output.

All sections of the resolution are present in the output, including the county header, the county seal (as an image with alt text), the resolution title, the preamble (WHEREAS clauses), the resolution statement, the adoption date, and the signature block.

The signature section at the bottom of the document has been converted into a table format. This table correctly includes rows for both signees (Taylor McCurry and Steve Pitman), successfully capturing the intended information from the source. The headers 'Field' and 'Value' are acceptable AI-generated additions for structural accessibility.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: No significant portions of text within the preamble or resolution clauses are missing. The core message and legislative references are preserved throughout the conversion.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There is a minor typo in the first WHEREAS clause. The source text specifies the classification as 'Protective Service Occupations' (singular), while the output text incorrectly pluralizes it as 'Protective Services Occupations'. This alters the official name of the category being discussed in that paragraph.
checks.b_evidence_source: reclassify 911 dispatchers from “Office and Administrative Support” to “Protective Service Occupations”
checks.b_evidence_output: reclassify 911 dispatchers from "Office and Administrative Support" to "Protective Services Occupations"

checks.a_analysis: The output contains significant content duplication. The text elements in reading_order 1 through 11 accurately capture the document's text. However, Image 24 (reading_order 24) is an image capture of the entire document's text, effectively repeating all previously transcribed semantic content. Additionally, Image 15 duplicates the header text 'NORTH CAROLINA MITCHELL COUNTY' already found in reading_order 1 and 2.
checks.b_evidence_source: The text of the resolution and the header 'NORTH CAROLINA MITCHELL COUNTY' appear once in the source.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text is present semantically in elements 1-11 and visually duplicated in image elements 15 and 24.

checks.a_analysis: The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a standard progression. The main resolution title is assigned H2 (reading_order 3), and the secondary resolving clause is assigned H3 (reading_order 9). This is a valid hierarchy.

The signature block is converted into a table (reading_order 12). While the source does not use a formal table, representing the aligned signature lines as a table is a reasonable structural choice for preserving horizontal layout in a digital format. The generated headers 'Field' and 'Value' are permitted AI additions for accessibility.

The reading order is significantly altered for the document's primary visual identifier. The Mitchell County seal, which appears at the top center of the source document, is placed at the end of the output (reading_order 13), following the signatures. This misplacement disrupts the logical flow of identifying the issuing entity before the body of the text.
checks.b_evidence_source: The official seal is positioned at the top center of the page, above the resolution title.
checks.b_evidence_output: The image of the seal is assigned reading_order 13, appearing after the 'Adopted...' paragraph and the signature table.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any lists; therefore, no list conversion errors are present.
The source document contains no URLs or contact links, and none were fabricated or incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML version transforms the source's multi-aligned header and signature blocks into a single-column, responsive layout. While the Mitchell County seal has been moved from the top to the middle of the document, the overall logical flow (Heading -> Body Text -> Signatures) is maintained. The content is accessible and follows the intended order of information.

The source document does not contain data tables. The conversion has created a 'Signatures' table to represent the sign-off section. This table is correctly structured with clear headers and row labels. Although the signatures themselves are provided as images below the table rather than within the 'Value' cells, the table remains visually cohesive and legible.

All meaningful images, including the official seal and the handwritten signatures, are present in the HTML rendering. The signature images appear to be slightly clipped (e.g., the 'T' in Taylor is partially missing), but they remain recognizable as signatures and are functional representations of the original content.

The entire content of the resolution is visually duplicated within the document body. After the standard rendering of the text and signatures, the document repeats the entire content (Header, Body, and Signatures) in a high-contrast section with a black background. This is a clear case of genuine body content being repeated on the same page.
checks.b_evidence_source: The complete text of the resolution beginning with 'WHEREAS, in September of 2021...' through the final adoption date.
checks.b_evidence_output: The entire resolution text is repeated twice: once in the standard light-mode section and again in the high-contrast dark-mode section at the bottom of the page.
checks.b_evidence_location: Lower half of the HTML rendered page, starting with the heading 'MITCHELL COUNTY' on a black background.
